summary:
The term for when all progenitor cells are absent and no part of that particular tissue develops into any recognizable anatomical entity is termed 'agenesis'. This applies directly here given it describes full lack from initial cellular stages through final formation phase without exception across entire intended area/structure involved during embryonic growth processes resulting ultimately in absolute structural deficiency post birth if not corrected prenatally via medical intervention measures available today depending upon affected region/body system concerned amongst others factors influencing prognosis outcomes overall severity levels etc.. To reiterate succinctly but comprehensively:

Agenesisis defined by the complete failure of developmental process starting right after conception stage till end point maturity level reached naturally under normal circumstances barring external interferences affecting natural course progression normally expected within healthy individuals free from genetic predispositions known risk categories identified prior diagnosis confirmation made possible only once thorough evaluation conducted thoroughly examining multiple aspects including imaging studies alongside detailed patient history reviews among many more diagnostic tools utilized collectively towards achieving accurate assessment results reliably reflecting true nature underlying etiology responsible causative agents driving observed anomalies noted clinically observable manifestations presented currently being evaluated herein..
